generatedSentence: In the darkest outer sectors, a looming gentrification project threatened the forever peace, led by a wastrel ruler whose capricious laws and projected lovely imagery by veejays masked a system built to subjugate the populace with economic violence. Sir Kael, one of the last true knights, felt an excited urgency as he observed the biggest corporate entity's slow advance, a flyback to ancient tyrannies, its oil wells expanding, signaling a terrifying ending for his flock, causing him to blink with resolve, knowing he couldn't let their future fly away like a phantom valentine, despite the bark of robotic guards and the oppressive digital hose of propaganda, all while merchants flaunted their jewelry with an unsettling velocity. Kael, his old armor now clunky and made of cotton beneath an acrylic overlay, felt a calcified fear, a heavy stone in his lungs, as he plotted his jailbreak from the city's inward spiraling despair, aiming for the pinnacle of the antagonist's stronghold. His ally, a virtuous but flawed hacker with a notorious past in ufology theories, provided crucial parameter data and an intricate plotter map of the scaffolding leading to the vault, where the tyrant kept the city's power lock – a resource he’d recycle from the poor. Kael’s objective was clear: to verify the rumors of a dangerous leviathan of a weapon, an integral part of the regime’s power, even if it meant a direct confrontation with the corrupt sheriff and his tacticity drones. The climb was a neverending ordeal, his body jerking with exhaustion, sweat beading on his brow as he dodged the tentacles of the security network, his longing for success fueling his every jumping move. He knew the plotter's schematic showed a central radius of influence, a takeaway from his ally’s data, and he pushed on, panting, past the artics where the city's biggest generators hummed, until he reached the final entrances. There, a group of elite guards, like trolls from a dark fantasy, awaited, their bayonet tips gleaming under the cinematic lighting, and Kael, despite his unlucky streak and the normal human suffers he endured, understood that this was the climax, the picture he had seen so many once in his mind's eye; he would not fabricate a retreat, nor be a crybaby, for his kinsman were depending on him. In the ensuing violence, Kael sustained an injured arm, a sickly pain, but his will remained unbroken, his earnings measured in saved lives, not coin, as he disrupted the diffraction shield and activated the emergency flyback sequence, creating a dazzling display like fireworks. The tyrannical machine, now a freaky mess, suffered an instant infection, its systems collapsing until the city's power grid was safe, no longer mourning its former state. Though his body was flawed, and he would need a doctor for the radiation burns, Kael looked at the symbolic sunflower that bloomed in the restored square, a jewel of hope, understanding that the city's nickname could now be 'Freeport', and he allowed himself to linger, watching the merchants with their ultrasounds check the new, healthy growth, a sign of their renewed forever.
panelSentences: In the darkest outer sectors, a looming gentrification project threatened the forever peace, led by a wastrel ruler whose capricious laws and projected lovely imagery by veejays masked a system built to subjugate the populace with economic violence. Sir Kael, one of the last true knights, felt an excited urgency as he observed the biggest corporate entity's slow advance, a flyback to ancient tyrannies, its oil wells expanding, signaling a terrifying ending for his flock, causing him to blink with resolve, knowing he couldn't let their future fly away like a phantom valentine, despite the bark of robotic guards and the oppressive digital hose of propaganda, all while merchants flaunted their jewelry with an unsettling velocity.

Kael, his old armor now clunky and made of cotton beneath an acrylic overlay, felt a calcified fear, a heavy stone in his lungs, as he plotted his jailbreak from the city's inward spiraling despair, aiming for the pinnacle of the antagonist's stronghold. His ally, a virtuous but flawed hacker with a notorious past in ufology theories, provided crucial parameter data and an intricate plotter map of the scaffolding leading to the vault, where the tyrant kept the city's power lock – a resource he’d recycle from the poor. Kael’s objective was clear: to verify the rumors of a dangerous leviathan of a weapon, an integral part of the regime’s power, even if it meant a direct confrontation with the corrupt sheriff and his tacticity drones.

The climb was a neverending ordeal, his body jerking with exhaustion, sweat beading on his brow as he dodged the tentacles of the security network, his longing for success fueling his every jumping move. He knew the plotter's schematic showed a central radius of influence, a takeaway from his ally’s data, and he pushed on, panting, past the artics where the city's biggest generators hummed, until he reached the final entrances. There, a group of elite guards, like trolls from a dark fantasy, awaited, their bayonet tips gleaming under the cinematic lighting, and Kael, despite his unlucky streak and the normal human suffers he endured, understood that this was the climax, the picture he had seen so many once in his mind's eye; he would not fabricate a retreat, nor be a crybaby, for his kinsman were depending on him.

In the ensuing violence, Kael sustained an injured arm, a sickly pain, but his will remained unbroken, his earnings measured in saved lives, not coin, as he disrupted the diffraction shield and activated the emergency flyback sequence, creating a dazzling display like fireworks. The tyrannical machine, now a freaky mess, suffered an instant infection, its systems collapsing until the city's power grid was safe, no longer mourning its former state. Though his body was flawed, and he would need a doctor for the radiation burns, Kael looked at the symbolic sunflower that bloomed in the restored square, a jewel of hope, understanding that the city's nickname could now be 'Freeport', and he allowed himself to linger, watching the merchants with their ultrasounds check the new, healthy growth, a sign of their renewed forever.
rawPanelSentences: In the **darkest outer** sectors, a looming **gentrification** project threatened the **forever** peace, led by a **wastrel** ruler whose **capricious** laws and projected **lovely** imagery by **veejays** masked a system built to **subjugate** the populace with economic **violence**. Sir Kael, one of the last true **knights**, felt an **excited** urgency as he observed the **biggest** corporate entity's slow advance, a **flyback** to ancient tyrannies, its **oil** wells expanding, signaling a terrifying **ending** for his **flock**, causing him to **blink** with resolve, knowing he couldn't let their future **fly** away like a phantom **valentine**, despite the **bark** of robotic guards and the oppressive digital **hose** of propaganda, all while merchants flaunted their **jewelry** with an unsettling **velocity**.

Kael, his old armor now **clunky** and made of **cotton** beneath an **acrylic** overlay, felt a **calcified** fear, a heavy stone in his **lungs**, as he plotted his **jailbreak** from the city's **inward** spiraling despair, aiming for the **pinnacle** of the antagonist's stronghold. His **ally**, a **virtuous** but **flawed** hacker with a **notorious** past in **ufology** theories, provided crucial **parameter** data and an intricate **plotter** map of the **scaffolding** leading to the vault, where the tyrant kept the city's power **lock** – a resource he’d **recycle** from the poor. Kael’s **objective** was clear: to **verify** the rumors of a **dangerous leviathan** of a weapon, an **integral** part of the regime’s power, even if it meant a direct confrontation with the corrupt **sheriff** and his **tacticity** drones.

The climb was a **neverending** ordeal, his body **jerking** with exhaustion, sweat beading on his brow as he dodged the **tentacles** of the security network, his **longing** for success fueling his every **jumping** move. He knew the **plotter**'s schematic showed a central **radius** of influence, a **takeaway** from his **ally**’s data, and he pushed on, **panting**, past the **artics** where the city's **biggest** generators hummed, until he reached the final **entrances**. There, a **group** of elite guards, like **trolls** from a dark **fantasy**, awaited, their **bayonet** tips gleaming under the **cinematic lighting**, and Kael, despite his **unlucky** streak and the **normal** human **suffers** he endured, understood that this was the **climax**, the **picture** he had seen so many **once** in his mind's eye; he would not **fabricate** a retreat, nor be a **crybaby**, for his **kinsman** were depending on him.

In the ensuing **violence**, Kael sustained an **injured** arm, a **sickly** pain, but his will remained unbroken, his **earnings** measured in saved lives, not coin, as he disrupted the **diffraction** shield and activated the emergency **flyback** sequence, creating a dazzling display like **fireworks**. The tyrannical machine, now a **freaky** mess, suffered an instant **infection**, its systems collapsing **until** the city's power grid was safe, no longer **mourning** its former state. Though his body was **flawed**, and he would need a **doctor** for the **radiation** burns, Kael looked at the symbolic **sunflower** that bloomed in the restored square, a **jewel** of hope, understanding that the city's **nickname** could now be 'Freeport', and he allowed himself to **linger**, watching the **merchants** with their **ultrasounds** check the new, healthy growth, a sign of their renewed **forever**.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A knight in worn, dark-chrome power armor with glowing cyan accents and a tattered crimson cape. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Sir Kael stands on a rain-slicked rooftop, looking down at a sprawling neon-drenched cityscape, a vast corporate leviathan structure dominating the horizon, viewed from a high-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, in a cramped, dark server room, furiously typing on a holographic keyboard, surrounded by glowing data streams, with a mid-shot framing him intensely focused. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael, wielding an energy bayonet, engaged in a dynamic, low-angle combat pose against several robotic guards on a precarious scaffolding high above the city, amidst sparks and explosions. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, injured and panting, leans against a wall, watching the corporate leviathan's central core explode with a silent, dazzling light, a triumphant but weary close-up on his face.

definitions.definition: The movement of the electron beam back to its initial position between scan lines in a cathode-ray tube display.
A special kind of electrical transformer, originally designed to generate high-voltage sawtooth signals at a relatively high frequency, and now extensively used in switched-mode power supplies.
An extremely long time.
definitions.example: I haven't seen him in forever!

definitions.definition: A mythical time in the infinite future that will never come.
definitions.example: Sure, I'd be happy to meet with you on the 12th of forever.

definitions.definition: Permanent, lasting
(duration) For all time, for all eternity; for a lifetime; for an infinite amount of time.
definitions.example: I shall love you forever.

definitions.definition: (duration) For a very long time, a seeming eternity.
definitions.example: We had to wait forever to get inside.

definitions.definition: (frequency) Constantly or frequently.
definitions.example: You are forever nagging me.

definitions.definition: A large number of birds, especially those gathered together for the purpose of migration.
A large number of animals, especially sheep or goats kept together.
Those served by a particular pastor or shepherd.
A large number of people.
A religious congregation.
To congregate in or head towards a place in large numbers.
definitions.example: People flocked to the cinema to see the new film.

definitions.definition: To flock to; to crowd.
To treat a pool with chemicals to remove suspended particles.
The short, loud, explosive sound uttered by a dog, a fox, and some other animals.
An abrupt loud vocal utterance.
To make a short, loud, explosive noise with the vocal organs (said of animals, especially dogs).
definitions.example: The neighbour's dog is always barking.

definitions.definition: To make a clamor; to make importunate outcries.
To speak sharply.
definitions.example: The sergeant barked an order.

definitions.definition: (urban studies) The renewal and rebuilding that accompanies the influx of middle class or affluent people into deteriorating areas and often displaces earlier, usually poorer, residents; any example of such a process.
One who is profligate, who wastes time or resources extravagantly.
A neglected child.
Refuse; rubbish.
Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
definitions.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.

definitions.definition: To stir the emotions of.
definitions.example: The fireworks which opened the festivities excited anyone present.

definitions.definition: To arouse or bring out (e.g. feelings); to stimulate.
definitions.example: Favoritism tends to excite jealousy in the ones not being favored.

definitions.definition: To cause an electron to move to a higher than normal state; to promote an electron to an outer level.
definitions.example: By applying electric potential to the neon atoms, the electrons become excited, then emit a photon when returning to normal.

definitions.definition: To energize (an electromagnet); to produce a magnetic field in.
definitions.example: to excite a dynamo

definitions.definition: Having great enthusiasm.
definitions.example: He was very excited about his promotion.

definitions.definition: Being in a state of higher energy.
definitions.example: The excited electrons give off light when they drop to a lower energy state.

definitions.definition: Having an erection; erect.
Sexually aroused.
A termination or conclusion.
The last part of something.
(grammar) The last morpheme of a word, added to some base to make an inflected form (such as -ing in "ending").
A vector quantity that denotes the rate of change of position with respect to time, or a speed with the directional component.
Rapidity of motion.
The rate of occurrence.
The number of times that an average unit of currency is spent during a specific period of time.
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
Vagina, vulva.
A young servant or follower; a trained military attendant in service of a lord.
A minor nobleman with an honourable military rank who had served as a page and squire.
(by extension) An armored and mounted warrior of the Middle Ages.
definitions.example: King Arthur and the Knights of the Round Table

definitions.definition: (modern) A person on whom a knighthood has been conferred by a monarch.
A brave, chivalrous and honorable man devoted to a noble cause or love interest.
A chess piece, often in the shape of a horse's head, that is moved two squares in one direction and one at right angles to that direction in a single move, leaping over any intervening pieces.
A playing card bearing the figure of a knight; the knave or jack.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Ypthima.
(modern) A generic name for various mushrooms belonging to the fungal order gilled mushrooms; scientific name Tricholoma.
Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
definitions.example: The room was too dark for reading.

definitions.definition: (of colour) Dull or deeper in hue; not bright or light.
definitions.example: my sister's hair is darker than mine;  her skin grew dark with a suntan

definitions.definition: Hidden, secret, obscure.
Without moral or spiritual light; sinister, malign.
definitions.example: a dark villain;  a dark deed

definitions.definition: Conducive to hopelessness; depressing or bleak.
definitions.example: the Great Depression was a dark time;  the film was a dark psychological thriller

definitions.definition: Lacking progress in science or the arts; said of a time period.
With emphasis placed on the unpleasant aspects of life; said of a work of fiction, a work of nonfiction presented in narrative form or a portion of either.
definitions.example: The ending of this book is rather dark.

definitions.definition: A supernatural being of varying size, now especially a grotesque humanoid creature living in caves or hills or under bridges.
An ugly person of either sex, especially one seeking sexual experiences.

Optical ejections from the top of the electrically active core regions of thunderstorms that are red in color that seem to occur after tendrils of vigorous sprites extend downward toward the cloud tops.
An instance of trolling, especially, in fishing, the trailing of a baited line.
A person who provokes others (chiefly on the Internet) for their own personal amusement or to cause disruption.
To saunter.
To trundle, to roll from side to side.
To draw someone or something out, to entice, to lure as if with trailing bait.

To fish in; to try to catch fish from.

definitions.definition: (to post inflammatory material so as) to attempt to lure others into combative argument for purposes of personal entertainment and/or gratuitous disruption, especially in an online community or discussion

definitions.definition: An attractive, lovely person, especially a (professional) beauty.

definitions.definition: (of a city) populous

definitions.example: Such an extravagant valentine was unexpected.

definitions.definition: A person to whom a valentine is given or from whom it is received, especially on February 14th.

definitions.definition: A rotational or twisting effect of a force; a moment of force, defined for measurement purposes as an equivalent straight line force multiplied by the distance from the axis of rotation (SI unit newton metre or Nm; imperial unit pound-foot or lb·ft, not to be confused with the foot pound-force, commonly "foot-pound", a unit of work or energy)
To twist or turn something.
Hardened from the deposit of calcium salts.

A number, the limit of the sums computed in a process in which the domain of a function is divided into small subsets and a possibly nominal value of the function on each subset is multiplied by the measure of that subset, all these products then being summed.
definitions.example: The integral of x\mapsto x^2 on [0,1] is \frac{1}{3}.

definitions.definition: A definite integral, a limit of sums.
Antiderivative
definitions.example: The integral of x^2 is \frac{x^3}{3} plus a constant.

Made of cotton.

Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.
definitions.example: Don't pick at that scab.

definitions.definition: To harvest a fruit or vegetable for consumption by removing it from the plant to which it is attached; to harvest an entire plant by removing it from the ground.
definitions.example: It's time to pick the tomatoes.

definitions.definition: To pull apart or away, especially with the fingers; to pluck.
definitions.example: She picked flowers in the meadow.

definitions.definition: To take up; especially, to gather from here and there; to collect; to bring together.
definitions.example: to pick rags

definitions.definition: To remove something from somewhere with a pointed instrument, with the fingers, or with the teeth.
definitions.example: to pick the teeth; to pick a bone; to pick a goose; to pick a pocket

definitions.definition: To decide upon, from a set of options; to select.
definitions.example: I'll pick the one with the nicest name.

definitions.definition: To seek (a fight or quarrel) where the opportunity arises.
To recognise the type of ball being bowled by a bowler by studying the position of the hand and arm as the ball is released.
definitions.example: He didn't pick the googly, and was bowled.

definitions.definition: To pluck the individual strings of a musical instrument or to play such an instrument.
definitions.example: He picked a tune on his banjo.

definitions.definition: To open (a lock) with a wire, lock pick, etc.
To eat slowly, sparingly, or by morsels; to nibble.
To do anything fastidiously or carefully, or by attending to small things; to select something with care.
definitions.example: I gingerly picked my way between the thorny shrubs.

definitions.definition: To steal; to pilfer.
To throw; to pitch.
To peck at, as a bird with its beak; to strike at with anything pointed; to act upon with a pointed instrument; to pierce; to prick, as with a pin.
To separate or open by means of a sharp point or points.
definitions.example: to pick matted wool, cotton, oakum, etc.

definitions.definition: To screen.
A gathering to pick fruit.
definitions.example: We went to a strawberry picking last June.

definitions.definition: (usually pluralized) Items remaining after others have selected the best; scraps, as of food.
(usually pluralized) Income or other gains, especially if obtained in an unscrupulous or objectionable manner.
Something picked or pulled out.
definitions.example: The schoolboy flicked his nose pickings across the classroom.

definitions.definition: The act of making a choice; selection.
The final finishing of woven fabrics by removing burs, etc.
The removal of defects from electrotype plates.
Dabbing in stoneworking.
The highest point.
A tall, sharp and craggy rock or mountain.
An all-time high; a point of greatest achievement or success.
An upright member, generally ending in a small spire, used to finish a buttress, to constitute a part in a proportion, as where pinnacles flank a gable or spire.
To put something on a pinnacle.
To build or furnish with a pinnacle or pinnacles.
A biological organ of vertebrates that controls breathing and oxygenates the blood.
(plural) Capacity for exercise or exertion; breath.
definitions.example: He hasn't the lungs to play long rallies like he use to.

definitions.definition: That which supplies oxygen or fresh air, such as trees, parklands, forest, etc., to a place.
A motor vehicle for transporting large numbers of people along roads.
An electrical conductor or interface serving as a common connection for two or more circuits or components.
(medical industry) An ambulance.
A main, direct public road, especially a multi-lane, high speed thoroughfare connecting major population centers.
Any public road for vehicular traffic.
A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
definitions.example: A group of people gathered in front of the Parliament to demonstrate against the Prime Minister's proposals.

definitions.definition: A set with an associative binary operation, under which there exists an identity element, and such that each element has an inverse.
An effective divisor on a curve.
A (usually small) group of people who perform music together.
definitions.example: Did you see the new jazz group?

definitions.definition: A small number (up to about fifty) of galaxies that are near each other.
A column in the periodic table of chemical elements.
A functional group.
definitions.example: Nitro is an electron-withdrawing group.

definitions.definition: A subset of a culture or of a society.
An air force formation.
A collection of formations or rock strata.
A number of users with same rights with respect to accession, modification, and execution of files, computers and peripherals.
An element of an espresso machine from which hot water pours into the portafilter.
A number of eighth, sixteenth, etc., notes joined at the stems; sometimes rather indefinitely applied to any ornament made up of a few short notes.
A set of teams playing each other in the same division, while not during the same period playing any teams that belong to other sets in the division.
A commercial organization.
To put together to form a group.
definitions.example: group the dogs by hair colour

definitions.definition: To come together to form a group.
To cause irritation or deep bitterness.
To fester.
definitions.example: a splinter rankles in the flesh

definitions.definition: A material object that physically exists.
A goal that is striven for.
(grammar) The objective case.
(grammar) a noun or pronoun in the objective case.
The lens or lenses of a camera, microscope, or other optical device closest to the object being examined.
Of or relating to a material object, actual existence or reality.
Not influenced by the emotions or prejudices.
Based on observed facts.
(grammar) Of, or relating to a noun or pronoun used as the object of a verb.
(grammar) Of, or relating to verbal conjugation that indicates the object (patient) of an action. (In linguistic descriptions of Tundra Nenets, among others.)
A process of choosing a leader, members of parliament, councillors or other representatives by popular vote.
definitions.example: How did you vote in (UK also: at) the last election?

definitions.definition: The choice of a leader or representative by popular vote.
definitions.example: The election of John Smith was due to his broad appeal.

definitions.definition: An option that is selected.

definitions.definition: Any conscious choice.
In Calvinism, God's predestination of saints including all of the elect.
Those who are elected.
Full of virtue, having excellent moral character.
definitions.example: Successful communities need strong, selfless leaders and a virtuous people.

definitions.definition: The relative stereochemistry of adjacent chiral centers within a macromolecule.
An elongated, boneless, flexible organ or limb of some animals, such as the octopus and squid.
One of the glandular hairs on the leaves of certain insectivorous plants.
An insidious reach or influence.
definitions.example: the tentacles of the criminal underworld

definitions.definition: A restaurant that sells food to be eaten elsewhere.
definitions.example: If you're hungry, there's a takeaway just around the corner.

definitions.definition: A meal bought to be eaten elsewhere.
definitions.example: I fancy an Indian takeaway tonight.

definitions.definition: The preliminary part of a golfer′s swing when the club is brought back away from the ball.
A concession made by a labor union in the course of negotiations.
(frequently plural) An idea from a talk, presentation, etc., that the listener or reader should remember and consider.
(Of food) intended to be eaten off the premises from which it was bought.
definitions.example: I couldn't be bothered cooking, so I bought a takeaway curry.

definitions.definition: A representation of anything (as a person, a landscape, a building) upon canvas, paper, or other surface, by drawing, painting, printing, photography, etc.
An image; a representation as in the imagination.
A painting.
definitions.example: There was a picture hanging above the fireplace.

definitions.definition: A photograph.
definitions.example: I took a picture of the church.

definitions.definition: A motion picture.
definitions.example: Casablanca is my all-time favorite picture.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) ("the pictures") Cinema (as a form of entertainment).
definitions.example: Let's go to the pictures.

definitions.definition: A paragon, a perfect example or specimen (of a category).
definitions.example: She's the very picture of health.

definitions.definition: An attractive sight.
definitions.example: The garden is a real picture at this time of year.

definitions.definition: The art of painting; representation by painting.
A figure; a model.
Situation.
definitions.example: The employment picture for the older middle class is not so good.

definitions.definition: To represent in or with a picture.
To imagine or envision.
To depict or describe vividly.

A pointed instrument of the dagger kind fitted on the muzzle of a musket or rifle, so as to give the soldier increased means of offence and defence. Originally, the bayonet was made with a handle, which needed to be fitted into the bore of the musket after the soldier had fired.
A pin which plays in and out of holes made to receive it, and which thus serves to engage or disengage parts of the machinery.
To stab with a bayonet.
To compel or drive by the bayonet.
The shooting forth of anything from a point or surface, like diverging rays of light.

definitions.definition: The process of radiating waves or particles.
The transfer of energy via radiation (as opposed to convection or conduction).
Radioactive energy.
(frequency) One and only one time.
definitions.example: I have only once eaten pizza.

definitions.definition: (temporal location) Formerly; during some period in the past.
definitions.example: He was once the most handsome man around.

definitions.definition: Multiplied by one: indicating that a number is multiplied by one.
definitions.example: Once three is three.

definitions.definition: As soon as.
At a future time.
As soon as; when; after.
definitions.example: Once he is married, he will be able to claim the inheritance.

definitions.definition: The action of entering, or going in.
definitions.example: Her entrance attracted no attention whatsoever.

definitions.definition: The act of taking possession, as of property, or of office.

definitions.definition: The place of entering, as a gate or doorway.

definitions.definition: The right to go in.

definitions.definition: The entering upon; the beginning, or that with which the beginning is made; the commencement; initiation.

definitions.definition: The causing to be entered upon a register, as a ship or goods, at a customhouse; an entering.

definitions.definition: The angle which the bow of a vessel makes with the water at the water line.
The bow, or entire wedgelike forepart of a vessel, below the water line.
When a musician starts playing or singing, entry.
To substantiate or prove the truth of something
To confirm or test the truth or accuracy of something
To affirm something formally, under oath
A male relative.
Full of danger.

definitions.definition: Causing danger; ready to do harm or injury.
In a condition of danger, as from illness; threatened with death.
Hard to suit; difficult to please.
Reserved; not affable.
A vast sea monster of tremendous strength, described as the most powerful and dangerous creature in the ocean.
Something large; behemoth.

definitions.definition: Very large; gargantuan.
To form into a whole by uniting its parts; to construct; to build.

definitions.definition: To form by art and labor; to manufacture; to produce.

definitions.definition: To invent and form; to forge; to devise falsely.

definitions.definition: To cut up an animal as preparation for cooking, particularly used in reference to fowl.
State or condition of not meeting a desirable or intended objective, opposite of success.
An object, person or endeavour in a state of failure or incapable of success.
Termination of the ability of an item to perform its required function; breakdown.
Bankruptcy.
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.
definitions.example: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

definitions.definition: (in particular) To wish, desire or demand to see, have the presence of or do business with.
definitions.example: Danish police want him for embezzlement.

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.
definitions.example: You can leave if you want.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).
definitions.example: You’ll want to repeat this three or four times to get the best result.

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.

definitions.definition: To be in a state of destitution; to be needy; to lack.
definitions.example: The paupers desperately want.

definitions.definition: To lack and be without, to not have (something).

Wished for; desired; sought
Subject to immediate detainment by law enforcement authorities on sight.
An escape from prison.
To escape from prison.
To modify the firmware of an electronic device, especially a mobile phone, in order to remove restrictions that prevent it from running unofficial software.

Someone whose feelings are very easily hurt, often by trivial matters.
Someone who takes offense or excessively complains when things aren't going well.
A familiar, invented given name for a person or thing used instead of the actual name of the person or thing.
A kind of byname that describes a person by a characteristic of that person.
To give a nickname to (a person or thing).
To stay or remain in a place or situation, especially as if unwilling to depart or not easily able to do so; to loiter.
To remain alive or existent although still proceeding toward death or extinction; to die gradually.
(often followed by on) To consider or contemplate for a period of time; to engage in analytic thinking or discussion.
To set up a scaffolding; to surround a building with scaffolding.
To sustain; to provide support for.
To dispose of the bodies of the dead on a scaffold or raised platform, as by some Native American tribes.
A temporary modular system of tubes (or formerly wood) forming a framework used to support people and material in the construction or repair of buildings and other large structures.
Source code etc. that is incomplete and serves as a basis for further development.
Any framework or support.
One who rides racehorses competitively.
That part of a variable resistor or potentiometer that rides over the resistance wire
An operator of some machinery or apparatus.
A dealer in horses; a horse trader.
A cheat; one given to sharp practice in trade.
A prostitute's client.
A rapist.
To ride (a horse) in a race.
To jostle by riding against.

definitions.definition: To maneuver (something) by skill for one's advantage.
To cheat or trick.
A kind of gauze, or very thin silk.
Having no end or being endless.
A value kept constant during an experiment, equation, calculation or similar, but varied over other versions of the experiment, equation, calculation, etc.
A variable that describes some system (material, object, event etc.) or some aspect thereof
An input variable of a procedure definition, that gets an actual value (argument) at execution time (formal parameter).
definitions.example: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.

definitions.definition: An actual value given to such a formal parameter (argument or actual parameter).
A characteristic or feature that distinguishes something from others.
In the ellipse and hyperbola, a third proportional to any diameter and its conjugate, or in the parabola, to any abscissa and the corresponding ordinate.
definitions.example: The parameter of the principal axis of a conic section is called the latus rectum.

definitions.definition: The ratio of the three crystallographic axes which determines the position of any plane.
The fundamental axial ratio for a given species.
A person who plots.
An output device that draws graphs and other pictorial images on paper, sometimes using attached pens.
An instrument used to mark or find the position of a vessel on a chart.
To make a sudden uncontrolled movement.
To give a quick, often unpleasant tug or shake.
To masturbate.
To beat, to hit.
To throw with a quick and suddenly arrested motion of the hand.

definitions.definition: (usually transitive) To lift using a jerk.
To flout with contempt.
To cure (meat) by cutting it into strips and drying it, originally in the sun.
A motion that jerks; a jerk.
Wages, money earned, income.
Business profits.
Gains on investments; returns.
The bending of a wave around an obstacle.
The breaking up of an electromagnetic wave as it passes a geometric structure (e.g. a slit), followed by reconstruction of the wave by interference.
A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.
A person who is normal, who fits into mainstream society, as opposed to those who live alternative lifestyles.
The usual state.

definitions.definition: According to norms or rules or to a regular pattern.

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or being a solution containing one equivalent weight of solute per litre of solution.
Describing a straight chain isomer of an aliphatic hydrocarbon, or an aliphatic compound in which a substituent is in the 1- position of such a hydrocarbon.
(of a mode in an oscillating system) In which all parts of an object vibrate at the same frequency (see normal mode).
(of points) In the default position, set for the most frequently used route.
Perpendicular to a tangent of a curve or derivative of a surface.

definitions.definition: To breathe quickly or in a labored manner, as after exertion or from eagerness or excitement; to respire with heaving of the breast; to gasp.

The act of one who pants.
To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.

definitions.definition: An event or a display where fireworks are set off.
A boisterous or violent event or situation.

definitions.definition: The long bone in the forearm, on the side of the thumb.
The lighter bone (or fused portion of bone) in the forelimb of an animal.

The length of this line segment.
Anything resembling a radius, such as the spoke of a wheel, the movable arm of a sextant, or one of the radiating lines of a spider's web.

To take a long position in.
To await, aspire, desire greatly (something to occur or to be true)

definitions.definition: To be appropriate to, to pertain or belong to.
To belong.
Resembling a freak.
Odd; bizarre; unusual.

definitions.definition: Sexually deviant.

definitions.definition: To make (something) sickly.
To become sickly.
Frequently ill or in poor health.

definitions.definition: Not in good health; (somewhat) sick.
(of a plant) Characterized by poor or unhealthy growth.
Appearing ill, infirm or unhealthy; giving the appearance of illness.

definitions.definition: Shedding a relatively small amount of light; (of light) not very bright.
Lacking intensity or vigour.

Tending to produce nausea.

Marked by the occurrence of illness or disease (of a period of time).
Tending to produce disease or poor health.

definitions.definition: To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne.

definitions.definition: To pass by a spring or leap; to overleap.

To react to a sudden, often unexpected, stimulus (such as a sharp prick or a loud sound) by jerking the body violently.

definitions.definition: To increase sharply, to rise, to shoot up.

definitions.definition: To employ a move in certain board games where one game piece is moved from one legal position to another passing over the position of another piece.
definitions.example: The player's knight jumped the opponent's bishop.

definitions.definition: To move to a position (in a queue/line) that is further forward.

definitions.definition: To attack suddenly and violently.

definitions.definition: To engage in sexual intercourse with (a person).
To cause to jump.

definitions.definition: To move the distance between two opposing subjects.

To increase speed aggressively and without warning.
To expose to danger; to risk; to hazard.

To thicken or enlarge by endwise blows; to upset.

To flee; to make one's escape.
The act of performing a jump.
Exuberantly active; in full swing.
An articulated lorry.
An articulated bus.
To express sadness or sorrow for; to grieve over (especially a death).

To wear mourning.
The act of expressing or feeling sorrow or regret; lamentation.
Feeling or expressing sorrow over someone's death.
The traditional clothes worn by those who mourn (in Western societies, typically coloured black).
Drapes or coverings associated with mourning.

A physician; a member of the medical profession; one who is trained and licensed to heal the sick or injured. The final examination and qualification may award a doctor degree in which case the post-nominal letters are D.O., DPM, M.D., DMD, DDS, in the US or MBBS in the UK.
definitions.example: If you still feel unwell tomorrow, see your doctor.

A veterinarian; a medical practitioner who treats non-human animals.
A nickname for a person who has special knowledge or talents to manipulate or arrange transactions.
A teacher; one skilled in a profession or a branch of knowledge; a learned man.
Any mechanical contrivance intended to remedy a difficulty or serve some purpose in an exigency.

definitions.definition: A fish, the friar skate.
To act as a medical doctor to.

To make (someone) into an (academic) doctor; to confer a doctorate upon.
To physically alter (medically or surgically) a living being in order to change growth or behavior.

To take medicine.
Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
The act or process of infecting.

A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.

A precious or semi-precious stone; gem, gemstone.
A valuable object used for personal ornamentation, especially one made of precious metals and stones; a piece of jewellery.
Anything precious or valuable.

The clitoris.

Sound with a frequency greater than the upper limit of human hearing, which is approximately 20 kilohertz.
